spec_match.rationale: Direction (SecurityError → NotAllowedError) matches the feature title and the chromestatus framing; demo actually calls `navigator.credentials.create({publicKey, payment})` and surfaces `e.name`.

interactive.rationale: Two probe buttons (no-gesture, with-gesture) invoke the API live and compare the thrown `error.name` against the Chrome 135+ expectation, with an `actual result` readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb says `SecurityError now thrown for cross-origin / no-activation` — but the page actually shows NotAllowedError as the 135+ behaviour. The index blurb wording is therefore mis-stated, although the page itself is correct.

edge_cases.rationale: Probes the no-gesture path but only inside the same origin — the rename is specifically about cross-origin iframes without activation. The demo can't actually reproduce the original bug because it's running top-level, not in a cross-origin iframe.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables only, good/bad column semantics, no static-card. Button colour follows global rule.

openQuestions.title: Fix index blurb to match the page
openQuestions.detail: The feature index says `SecurityError now thrown for cross-origin / no-activation, matching the rest of WebAuthn` — but this concept page (correctly) shows the opposite: 135+ throws NotAllowedError. Update the index blurb to say `NotAllowedError now thrown for cross-origin / no-activation, replacing the historical SecurityError`.

openQuestions.title: Reproduce in a cross-origin iframe
openQuestions.detail: The bug fixed in 135 only manifests in a cross-origin iframe without activation. Running top-level, both pre-135 and 135+ Chrome throw NotAllowedError (no difference). To actually demonstrate the change, add an `<iframe sandbox="allow-scripts" src="https://other-origin.example/cred-create.html">` and have the iframe perform the call. The user can see the error.name differ pre/post 135.

openQuestions.title: Show the cross-feature impact on Secure Payment Confirmation
openQuestions.detail: SPC's authenticate() flow doesn't change, but merchants who catch SecurityError from a payment create() will now miss it. Add a link to the SPC spec section that describes this and call out the SecurityError catch as a likely migration breakage.

openQuestions.title: Test in WebAuthn virtual authenticator
openQuestions.detail: Chrome DevTools has a virtual authenticator panel (chrome://devtools/inspector/security). With it enabled, the gesture probe can succeed and complete the create — letting the demo show the full happy path alongside the error path.

summary: Correct direction (matches chromestatus), real API call, clean comparison panels. The main weakness is that the demo can't reproduce the original cross-origin-iframe bug from top-level, so it can't actually distinguish pre-135 from 135+ behaviour live. Add an iframe variant.
